requirements: Requirement 1 - Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
Requirement 2 - Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
Requirement 4 - Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
Requirement 5 - Protect All Systems Against Malware
Requirement 6 - Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
Requirement 7 - Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
Requirement 8 - Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
Requirement 9 - Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
Requirement 10 - Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
Requirement 11 - Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
Requirement 12 - Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
metadata.note: AWS is a PCI DSS Level 1 Service Provider. A shared responsibility model applies. Customers must ensure their use of AWS services is compliant within their own CDE (Cardholder Data Environment).

checks.name: CDE Isolated in Dedicated VPC
checks.description: Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) must be isolated in a dedicated VPC, separate from non-CDE workloads
checks.remediation: Create a dedicated VPC for CDE resources with no cross-VPC peering to non-CDE workloads

checks.name: Security Groups Restrict Inbound to CDE
checks.description: No security group allows unrestricted inbound access (0.0.0.0/0) to CDE systems on any port
checks.remediation: Restrict all security group inbound rules to known, required CIDRs and ports only

checks.name: Security Groups Deny All by Default
checks.description: All security groups deny inbound traffic by default; only explicitly required traffic is allowed
checks.remediation: Audit all security groups; remove any overly permissive rules

checks.name: VPC Flow Logs Enabled
checks.description: Enable VPC Flow Logs for all VPCs containing CDE resources to capture network traffic
checks.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs and send to CloudWatch Logs or S3 with 12-month retention

checks.name: CDE Resources in Private Subnets
checks.description: All CDE components (application servers, databases) must be deployed in private subnets
checks.remediation: Move CDE resources to private subnets; use ALB/NLB in public subnets as entry point

checks.name: WAF Enabled on Public-Facing Applications
checks.description: Deploy AWS WAF on all public-facing web applications that are part of or connected to the CDE
checks.remediation: Attach AWS WAF to CloudFront, ALB, or API Gateway endpoints serving CDE traffic

checks.name: Network ACLs Restrict CDE Traffic
checks.description: Use NACLs as an additional layer to restrict inbound and outbound traffic to the CDE
checks.remediation: Configure NACLs with explicit allow rules for required traffic; deny all others

checks.name: No Direct Internet Access to CDE
checks.description: CDE systems must not have direct inbound internet access; all internet-facing traffic routes through controlled entry points
checks.remediation: Remove internet gateways from private subnets; use NAT Gateway for outbound-only internet access

checks.name: No Default Credentials on CDE Systems
checks.description: All default vendor passwords and credentials must be changed before CDE systems are deployed
checks.remediation: Audit all CDE systems for default credentials; rotate immediately; enforce via IaC

checks.name: CDE Systems Hardened per Baseline
checks.description: All CDE system components are configured according to a documented hardening standard (CIS Benchmarks or equivalent)
checks.remediation: Apply CIS Benchmarks via AWS Systems Manager State Manager or golden AMIs

checks.name: Unnecessary Services Disabled on CDE Systems
checks.description: Only required ports, protocols, and services are enabled on CDE system components
checks.remediation: Disable unused services and ports via OS configuration and security groups

checks.name: AWS Config Enabled for CDE Accounts
checks.description: AWS Config must be enabled to continuously monitor CDE resource configurations
checks.remediation: Enable AWS Config with all resource types recorded in all CDE regions

checks.name: EC2 IMDSv2 Enforced
checks.description: Require IMDSv2 on all EC2 instances in the CDE to prevent SSRF-based credential theft
checks.remediation: Set HttpTokens to 'required' on all CDE EC2 instance metadata configurations

checks.name: No Storage of Sensitive Authentication Data Post-Authorization
checks.description: Full track data, CVV/CVC, and PINs must not be stored after transaction authorization
checks.remediation: Audit application code and databases; purge any stored SAD; implement tokenization

checks.name: PAN Masked When Displayed
checks.description: Primary Account Numbers (PAN) must be masked when displayed, showing only first 6 and last 4 digits
checks.remediation: Implement PAN masking in application display layer and APIs

checks.name: PAN Encrypted at Rest with Strong Cryptography
checks.description: Stored PAN must be rendered unreadable using strong cryptography (AES-256 or equivalent)
checks.remediation: Encrypt PAN using AES-256 with KMS CMK; use tokenization where possible

checks.name: S3 Encryption Enabled for CDE Buckets
checks.description: All S3 buckets in the CDE must use SSE-KMS with a customer-managed key
checks.remediation: Enable SSE-KMS on all CDE S3 buckets; enforce via bucket policy

checks.name: EBS Encryption Enabled for CDE Instances
checks.description: All EBS volumes attached to CDE EC2 instances must be encrypted at rest
checks.remediation: Enable EBS default encryption at account level; use KMS CMK

checks.name: RDS Encryption Enabled for CDE Databases
checks.description: All RDS/Aurora instances storing cardholder data must be encrypted at rest
checks.remediation: Enable RDS storage encryption with KMS CMK at instance creation

checks.name: Key Management Policy for Encryption Keys
checks.description: Cryptographic keys used to protect cardholder data must have documented key management procedures
checks.remediation: Use AWS KMS with documented key lifecycle, rotation, and access control procedures

checks.name: Cardholder Data Discovery Scan
checks.description: Perform periodic scans to detect unprotected PAN stored in unexpected locations
checks.remediation: Use Amazon Macie to discover and classify PAN in S3; scan databases and logs quarterly

checks.name: Amazon Macie Enabled for CDE S3 Buckets
checks.description: Enable Amazon Macie to automatically detect sensitive cardholder data in S3
checks.remediation: Enable Macie and configure custom data identifiers for PAN patterns

transmission_security.category: Requirement 4 - Protect Cardholder Data in Transit

checks.name: TLS 1.2+ Enforced on All CDE Endpoints
checks.description: All transmission of cardholder data over public networks must use TLS 1.2 or higher
checks.remediation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ on ALB, API Gateway, CloudFront; disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1

checks.name: S3 Buckets Enforce SSL-Only Access
checks.description: Deny HTTP (non-SSL) requests to CDE S3 buckets via bucket policy
checks.remediation: Add bucket policy denying s3:* when aws:SecureTransport is false

checks.name: Strong Cipher Suites Enforced
checks.description: Only strong, approved cipher suites are used on all CDE endpoints; weak ciphers are disabled
checks.remediation: Configure ALB/CloudFront security policies to use ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2 or equivalent

checks.name: No Cardholder Data Sent via Unencrypted Channels
checks.description: Cardholder data must never be transmitted via email, chat, or any unencrypted channel
checks.remediation: Implement DLP controls; audit application code for unencrypted data transmission

checks.name: Anti-Malware Solution on CDE Systems
checks.description: Deploy anti-malware on all CDE EC2 instances and endpoints that could be affected by malware
checks.remediation: Deploy Amazon GuardDuty malware protection and/or third-party AV via SSM

checks.name: GuardDuty Malware Protection Enabled
checks.description: Enable GuardDuty Malware Protection for EC2 instances in the CDE
checks.remediation: Enable GuardDuty with Malware Protection feature for CDE accounts

checks.name: Anti-Malware Definitions Kept Current
checks.description: Ensure anti-malware solutions are maintained with the latest definitions and signatures
checks.remediation: Configure automatic updates for anti-malware via SSM or vendor management console

checks.name: EC2 Instances Regularly Patched
checks.description: All CDE EC2 instances must be patched against known vulnerabilities promptly
checks.remediation: Use SSM Patch Manager with a compliant patch baseline; critical patches within 1 month

checks.name: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched Promptly
checks.description: Critical and high vulnerabilities in CDE systems must be patched within defined SLAs (e.g., critical within 30 days)
checks.remediation: Define patch SLAs; use AWS Inspector and SSM Patch Manager for tracking and remediation

checks.name: AWS Inspector Enabled for CDE Accounts
checks.description: Enable Amazon Inspector for continuous vulnerability scanning of CDE EC2 instances and container images
checks.remediation: Enable AWS Inspector v2 in all CDE accounts and regions

checks.name: WAF Active and Tuned for CDE Applications
checks.description: AWS WAF rules are actively maintained to protect CDE web applications from OWASP Top 10 attacks
checks.remediation: Deploy AWS Managed Rules and custom rules on WAF; review and tune regularly

checks.name: SAST/DAST in CI/CD Pipeline
checks.description: Static and dynamic application security testing is integrated into the CI/CD pipeline for CDE applications
checks.remediation: Integrate SAST tools (e.g., Semgrep, Checkmarx) and DAST tools into CI/CD pipelines

checks.name: Separation of CDE Environments
checks.description: Development, test, and production CDE environments must be separated using different AWS accounts or VPCs
checks.remediation: Use separate AWS accounts for dev/test/prod; enforce via AWS Organizations SCPs

checks.name: No Live PANs in Test Environments
checks.description: Real cardholder data (live PAN) must not be used in non-production environments
checks.remediation: Use synthetic or tokenized data in test environments; scan with Macie to detect real PAN

access_control.category: Requirement 7 - Restrict Access by Business Need to Know

checks.name: IAM Least Privilege for CDE Resources
checks.description: IAM policies for CDE resources must grant only the minimum permissions required
checks.remediation: Review and tighten IAM policies using IAM Access Analyzer; remove wildcard permissions

checks.name: No Shared IAM Credentials for CDE
checks.description: All access to CDE systems must use individual, unique credentials; no shared accounts
checks.remediation: Audit IAM users and roles; eliminate shared credentials; assign individual accounts

checks.name: Role-Based Access Control for CDE
checks.description: Access to cardholder data is based on documented roles with defined access needs
checks.remediation: Implement IAM roles mapped to job functions; document access matrix

checks.name: Quarterly Access Reviews for CDE
checks.description: Review all user access to CDE systems and cardholder data at least every 6 months
checks.remediation: Conduct formal access reviews; document and action revocations

checks.name: S3 CDE Buckets Block Public Access
checks.description: All S3 buckets in the CDE must block all forms of public access
checks.remediation: Enable S3 Block Public Access at account and bucket level for all CDE buckets

checks.name: RDS Not Publicly Accessible
checks.description: RDS instances in the CDE must not be publicly accessible
checks.remediation: Set PubliclyAccessible to false on all CDE RDS instances

checks.name: MFA Required for All CDE Access
checks.description: Multi-factor authentication must be required for all access into the CDE, including AWS Console and VPN
checks.remediation: Enforce MFA via IAM policy conditions and AWS Organizations SCPs for all CDE accounts

checks.name: MFA on Root Account
checks.description: MFA must be enabled on the AWS root account for all CDE accounts
checks.remediation: Enable hardware or virtual MFA on the root account; restrict root usage

checks.name: IAM Password Policy Enforced
checks.description: Enforce a strong IAM password policy: minimum 12 characters, complexity requirements, 90-day rotation
checks.remediation: Set IAM account password policy to meet PCI DSS minimum requirements

checks.name: IAM Access Keys Rotated Every 90 Days
checks.description: All IAM access keys providing access to CDE resources must be rotated every 90 days
checks.remediation: Rotate access keys on a 90-day schedule; enforce via AWS Config rule

checks.name: No Unused IAM Credentials
checks.description: Disable or remove IAM credentials that have not been used in 90 days
checks.remediation: Review IAM credential report monthly; disable stale credentials

checks.name: Automatic Session Timeout Configured
checks.description: CDE sessions must be locked after 15 minutes of inactivity
checks.remediation: Set IAM role session duration limits; enforce application-level idle timeout of 15 minutes

checks.name: Service Account Credentials Managed Securely
checks.description: Passwords and keys for service accounts must be stored in AWS Secrets Manager, not hardcoded
checks.remediation: Migrate all hardcoded credentials to AWS Secrets Manager; rotate automatically

logging_and_monitoring.category: Requirement 10 - Log and Monitor All Access

checks.name: CloudTrail Enabled in All CDE Regions
checks.description: CloudTrail must be enabled in all regions to log all API activity in the CDE
checks.remediation: Create a multi-region CloudTrail trail covering all management and data events

checks.name: CloudTrail Log File Integrity Validation
checks.description: Enable log file integrity validation to ensure audit logs have not been tampered with
checks.remediation: Enable log file validation on all CloudTrail trails

checks.name: CloudTrail Logs Encrypted with KMS
checks.description: Encrypt CloudTrail logs at rest using a KMS CMK
checks.remediation: Configure CloudTrail to use a KMS CMK for log encryption

checks.name: S3 Data Events Logged for CDE Buckets
checks.description: Enable CloudTrail data events for all S3 buckets in the CDE
checks.remediation: Enable S3 data events (read/write) in CloudTrail for all CDE buckets

checks.name: Log Retention Minimum 12 Months
checks.description: Audit logs must be retained for at least 12 months, with 3 months immediately available
checks.remediation: Set CloudWatch log groups and S3 lifecycle policies to retain logs for 12+ months

checks.name: Centralized Log Management (SIEM)
checks.description: Aggregate all CDE logs in a centralized SIEM for analysis and alerting
checks.remediation: Stream CloudTrail and VPC Flow Logs to a SIEM (e.g., Splunk, Elastic, Amazon Security Lake)

checks.name: Alerts for Critical Security Events
checks.description: Automated alerts must be configured for critical CDE events: root login, failed access, privilege escalation
checks.remediation: Create CloudWatch alarms and GuardDuty findings routed to SNS for immediate alerting

checks.name: Audit Log Access Restricted
checks.description: Access to CDE audit logs must be restricted to authorized personnel only; logs must be tamper-evident
checks.remediation: Apply S3 bucket policies and IAM restrictions on log buckets; enable S3 Object Lock

checks.name: S3 Object Lock on Log Buckets
checks.description: Use S3 Object Lock (WORM) on CloudTrail log buckets to prevent log deletion or modification
checks.remediation: Enable S3 Object Lock in compliance mode on all CloudTrail log destination buckets

checks.name: Quarterly Vulnerability Scans (ASV)
checks.description: Quarterly external vulnerability scans by an Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV) are required
checks.remediation: Engage a PCI SSC-approved ASV; schedule and complete quarterly external scans

checks.name: Annual Penetration Test of CDE
checks.description: Annual penetration testing of CDE network and application layers by qualified testers
checks.remediation: Engage qualified penetration testers annually; remediate all high/critical findings

checks.name: Internal Vulnerability Scans Quarterly
checks.description: Quarterly internal vulnerability scans of the CDE and all systems connected to it
checks.remediation: Use AWS Inspector or third-party scanner for quarterly internal scans

checks.name: Segmentation Testing Every 6 Months
checks.description: Penetration testing to verify CDE segmentation controls are effective at least every 6 months
checks.remediation: Include segmentation testing in pen test scope; document results and remediation

checks.name: IDS/IPS on CDE Network
checks.description: Intrusion detection/prevention systems must monitor all traffic at the CDE perimeter and within critical segments
checks.remediation: Deploy AWS Network Firewall or a third-party IDS/IPS solution on CDE network boundaries

checks.name: File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) on CDE Systems
checks.description: Deploy FIM solutions on CDE systems to detect unauthorized modification of critical files
checks.remediation: Deploy a FIM solution (e.g., AWS Systems Manager, Tripwire) on all CDE EC2 instances

checks.name: KMS CMK Used for CDE Encryption
checks.description: Use KMS customer-managed keys for all encryption of cardholder data; avoid AWS-managed keys where possible
checks.remediation: Create and use CMKs for all CDE data stores; document key custodians and procedures

checks.name: KMS Key Rotation Enabled
checks.description: Automatic annual key rotation must be enabled for all KMS CMKs used in the CDE
checks.remediation: Enable automatic key rotation on all CDE KMS CMKs

checks.name: Secrets Manager for All CDE Credentials
checks.description: All passwords, API keys, and database credentials for CDE systems must be stored in AWS Secrets Manager
checks.remediation: Migrate all hardcoded secrets to AWS Secrets Manager; enable automatic rotation

checks.name: GuardDuty Enabled in All CDE Regions
checks.description: Enable GuardDuty in all regions containing CDE resources for continuous threat detection
checks.remediation: Enable GuardDuty in all active CDE regions; configure SNS alerts for high-severity findings

checks.name: AWS Security Hub PCI DSS Standard Enabled
checks.description: Enable the PCI DSS security standard in AWS Security Hub for automated compliance checking
checks.remediation: Enable Security Hub and activate the PCI DSS v3.2.1/v4.0 standard

checks.name: Information Security Policy Documented and Reviewed Annually
checks.description: An overall information security policy that addresses all PCI DSS requirements must be documented and reviewed at least annually

checks.name: PCI DSS Scope Defined and Documented
checks.description: The CDE scope, including all in-scope systems, people, and processes, must be formally defined and documented

checks.name: Annual PCI DSS Scope Review
checks.description: PCI DSS scope must be reviewed and confirmed at least annually and after significant changes

checks.name: PCI DSS Responsibility Matrix Defined
checks.description: A responsibility matrix defining PCI DSS responsibility between AWS and the organization must be maintained

checks.name: Annual Formal Risk Assessment
checks.description: A formal risk assessment that identifies critical assets, threats, and vulnerabilities related to the CDE must be performed annually

checks.name: Third-Party / Vendor Risk Management
checks.description: All third-party vendors with access to the CDE or cardholder data must be assessed for PCI DSS compliance
evidence: Vendor PCI DSS AOC or questionnaire

checks.name: Service Provider List Maintained
checks.description: Maintain a list of all service providers with access to cardholder data, including their PCI DSS compliance status

checks.name: Incident Response Plan for CDE
checks.description: A documented incident response plan specific to CDE breaches and payment card compromises must exist

checks.name: IRP Tested Annually
checks.description: The incident response plan must be tested at least once per year

checks.name: 24/7 Incident Response Capability
checks.description: Personnel must be available 24/7 to respond to security incidents in the CDE

checks.name: Breach Notification Procedures
checks.description: Procedures for notifying card brands (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) and acquirer within required timeframes

checks.name: Annual PCI DSS Security Awareness Training
checks.description: All personnel with access to the CDE must complete security awareness training upon hire and annually

checks.name: Social Engineering / Phishing Training
checks.description: Training must include awareness of threats such as phishing and social engineering targeting CDE access

checks.name: Acceptable Use Policy Acknowledgment
checks.description: All personnel must acknowledge the acceptable use policy for CDE systems and cardholder data annually

checks.name: Formal Change Management Process for CDE
checks.description: All changes to CDE systems must go through a formal approval process before implementation

checks.name: Security Impact Assessment for CDE Changes
checks.description: Security impact of CDE changes must be assessed and documented before implementation
evidence: Change tickets with security review sign-off

checks.name: Production Deployment Approvals
checks.description: All production CDE deployments require code review and explicit sign-off before deployment

checks.name: Physical Access to CDE Systems Restricted
checks.description: Physical access to any on-premises CDE components must be restricted to authorized personnel

checks.name: Visitor Management Procedures
checks.description: Visitors accessing areas with CDE systems must be logged, badged, and escorted

checks.name: Media Containing Cardholder Data Secured
checks.description: All media containing cardholder data must be physically secured and access controlled

checks.name: Secure Destruction of Media with Cardholder Data
checks.description: Media containing cardholder data must be destroyed securely when no longer needed

checks.name: AWS Data Center Physical Security
checks.description: Verify AWS data center physical security via AWS PCI DSS AOC or SOC 2 report

checks.name: Annual PCI DSS Assessment (QSA or SAQ)
checks.description: Complete an annual PCI DSS assessment via a Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) or Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) as applicable

checks.name: Compensating Controls Documented
checks.description: Any compensating controls used in lieu of PCI DSS requirements must be formally documented and approved

checks.name: Executive Responsibility for PCI DSS Compliance
checks.description: A designated executive must be responsible for overall PCI DSS compliance and program management
